methods.description: Procedure for purchasing and selecting authentic pollen samples for analysis based on e-commerce sales ranking.

steps.description: Camellia pollen, lotus pollen, and rape pollen were purchased based on the ranking of sales from high to low on Taobao's website...
steps.searchable_summary: Purchase Camellia, lotus, and rape pollen from Taobao based on sales ranking.
inputs: Sales ranking data for pollen types

steps.description: ...and we chose the top five pollens in each ranking.
steps.searchable_summary: Select the top five pollens from each purchased type based on sales ranking.
outputs: 15 selected pollen samples (5 of each type)

methods.description: Analysis of acquired pollen samples for acetamiprid residues using Bic-ELISA and HPLC-MS/MS methods, and comparison of results against detection limits.

steps.description: According to the results of detection, neither the Bic-ELISA method... detected acetamiprid residues.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply the Bic-ELISA method to pollen samples to detect acetamiprid residues.
outputs: Bic-ELISA raw detection data/results for each sample

steps.searchable_summary: Apply the HPLC-MS/MS method to pollen samples to detect acetamiprid residues.
outputs: HPLC-MS/MS raw detection data/results for each sample

steps.description: Based on our research results, the linear detection range of acetamiprid was 5-200 ng/g in the HPLC-MS/MS method [32], and the linear detection range of acetamiprid was 2-200 ng/g in the Bic-ELISA method. That is to say, the residues of acetamiprid in 15 pollen samples were less than the detection range in both methods.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare detection results from Bic-ELISA and HPLC-MS/MS against their respective linear detection ranges to determine if residues are below the limit.
outputs: Conclusion on whether acetamiprid residues are above or below detection limits for each sample by each method
